sections.paragraph:
(from the root רוּם) f.-

(1) an oblation, offering, gift, Pro 29:4, אִישׁ תְּרוּמֹות “(a judge) who loves gifts;” especially used of a gift brought to the temple and the priests, Exo 25:2, Exo 25:3 30:13 , 14 Exo 30:14 Lev 7:32 22:12 . Hence שְׁדֵי תְרוּמֹות 2Sa 1:21, fields of offerings, i.e. very fertile fields, producing the best fruits, More fully תְּרוּמַת־יָד Deu 12:11, 17 Deu 12:17, and תְּרוּמַת־יְהֹוָה Exo 30:14, 15 Exo 30:15. See הֵרִים No. 3 .

(2) a heave-offering, compare תְּנוּפָה. Exo 29:27, שֹׁוק הַתְּרוּמָה the heave-shoulder; Lev 7:34, etc. [But see Thes. p. 1276.]
